cptDescriptions.78816: PET imaging for CT attenuation, whole body
cptDescriptions.78815: PET imaging for CT attenuation, limited area
cptDescriptions.78814: PET imaging with CT, limited area

icd10Descriptions.C50.911: Malignant neoplasm of unspecified site, right breast
icd10Descriptions.C50.912: Malignant neoplasm of unspecified site, left breast

criteria.description: Confirmed or strongly suspected malignancy with pathology report or imaging findings
Clinical indication: initial staging, restaging after treatment, or evaluation of suspected recurrence
Conventional imaging (CT, MRI) performed first and is insufficient for clinical decision-making
Requesting physician is oncologist, pulmonologist, or surgeon managing the malignancy
PET/CT will change the treatment plan (e.g., determine surgical candidacy, radiation field planning)

urgentBypass: Suspected brain metastases with acute neurological decline — authorize emergent imaging.
denialReasons: Requesting provider is not managing oncologist
Surveillance PET within 3 months of prior PET without clinical change
Indication is routine surveillance with no symptoms of recurrence
